DataQualityAssertionsDatasetFacet.description: list of tests performed on dataset or dataset columns, and their results

success.description: Whether the test found issues: 'true' (no issues found), 'false' (issues found). Independent of severity - a test can fail without blocking the pipeline when severity is 'warn'.

column.description: Column that test refers to. It should match the name provided in SchemaDatasetFacet. If column field is empty, then test refers to whole dataset.

severity.description: The configured severity level of the assertion. Common values are 'error' (test failure blocks pipeline) or 'warn' (test failure produces warning only).

description.description: Human-readable description of what the assertion checks.
description.example: Ensures all order IDs are unique across the table.

expected.description: The expected value or threshold for the assertion, serialized as a string.

actual.description: The actual value observed during the assertion, serialized as a string.

content.description: The assertion body, e.g. a SQL query or expression.
content.example: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE amount < 0

contentType.description: The format of the content field, allowing consumers to interpret or filter assertion content. Common values include 'sql', 'json', 'expression'.

params.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs for assertion-specific inputs.
